frames.name: Standing Light Punch
frames.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK

frames.name: Standing Medium Punch
frames.notes: 2f extra recovery on whiff; useful combo tool with a Target Combo follow-up; extends a wider body hurtbox until the end of recovery

frames.name: Standing Heavy Punch
frames.notes: Forces stand on hit (allowing Target Combo follow-up to work on crouching opponents); good poke with hitbox priority, many active frames, and a reactable hitconfirm window

frames.name: Standing Light Kick
frames.notes: Good range for a 5f light (useful in punishes and neutral buffers); DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

frames.name: Standing Medium Kick
frames.notes: Solid poke; front leg hurtbox is raised on frames 4-11 (can avoid low pokes)

frames.name: Standing Heavy Kick
frames.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents unless they extend a vertical hurtbox; 2 extra recovery frames on block; good horizontal anti-air that puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state (higher launch on Punish Counter juggle); {{sf6-adv|VP|+11}} before Punish Counter crumple state goes airborne; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

frames.name: Crouching Light Punch
frames.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

frames.name: Crouching Medium Punch
frames.notes: 2f extra recovery on whiff; combo and pressure tool; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-9 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air); extends a wider hurtbox until the end of recovery

frames.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
frames.notes: Forces stand on hit; decent anti-air; cannot hit cross-ups; fist hurtbox is invincible to air strikes on frame 9; head hurtbox is invincible to air strikes on frames 10-14; only 1st active frame is cancelable (extends a combo-only hitbox to match the range of the 2nd active frame for combo consistency); () refers to hit/block recovery on 1st active frame only; 5f less hitstun on active frames 2-5 (can be punishable on meaty timing)

frames.name: Crouching Light Kick
frames.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK

frames.name: Crouching Medium Kick
frames.notes: Range: 1.482; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

frames.name: Low Spinning Sweep
frames.notes: Good range and hitbox priority; has some juggle potential; on block, always plays out a fixed 29f recovery (cannot be made safer with meaty timing)

frames.name: Jumping Light Punch
frames.notes: Can be used as a fuzzy instant overhead

frames.name: Jumping Medium Punch
frames.notes: Both hits are cancelable to Air Tatsu and put airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; if 1st hit is stand blocked, the 2nd hit will not hit overhead against crouch block; lower body projectile invuln 8-22f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

frames.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
frames.notes: Spike knockdown on counter hit/Punish Counter vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

frames.name: Jumping Light Kick
frames.notes: Cross-up

frames.name: Jumping Medium Kick
frames.notes: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

frames.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
frames.notes: Strong jump-in from mid to long range; lower body projectile invuln 10-17f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

frames.name: Collarbone Breaker
frames.notes: Counter-hit/Punish Counter/Drive Rush frame advantage applies to both hits; 2nd hit is no longer an overhead if the first hit connects; up to +5 on hit with meaty timing for a meterless combo extension

frames.name: Solar Plexus Strike
frames.notes: 2 hits; good forward-moving combo starter and pressure tool; if 1st hit connects on an extended hurtbox, becomes {{sf6-adv|VP|+6}} on Counter-hit or {{sf6-adv|VP|+8}} on Punish Counter; Counter-hit/Punish Counter frame advantage applies to both hits; can be very advantageous if 1st hit trades with a light normal; 1st hit puts opponent into free juggle state so that the 2nd hit connects (to prevent Ryu from being punishable)

frames.name: Short Uppercut
frames.notes: Forces stand on hit; only 1st active frame is cancelable; () refers to hit/block recovery on 1st active frame only; extremely short range makes it finicky in combos; 8f less hitstun on active frames 2-4 (can be punishable with meaty timing)

frames.name: Axe Kick
frames.notes: () refers to 2nd hit startup; 1st hit forces stand and puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; 2nd hit puts airborne opponents into OTG bounce state; both hits are cancelable; Counter-hit and Punish Counter frame advantage applies to both hits

frames.name: Whirlwind Kick
frames.notes: Cancelable to regular/OD Air Tatsu on hit/block; Low Crush on frames 13-20 (not airborne); high pushback on block; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; good juggle option after midscreen OD High Blade Kick for corner carry and oki; proximity guard box begins on frame 2 and reaches the maximum attack range (prevents opponents from walking back out of the attack startup)

frames.name: Fuwa Triple Strike 1
frames.notes: Hits consistently at max 5MP range, but the LK can whiff if 5MP connects on an extended hurtbox; can be spaced safely against most characters for a spacing trap

frames.name: Fuwa Triple Strike 2
frames.notes: (refers to scaled damage); 6f gap before HK on block; cancels to Denjin Charge by inputting 2P on hit only (KD Adv. +7)

frames.name: High Double Strike
frames.notes: HK cannot hit vs. crouch block (much more unsafe); cancels to Denjin Charge by inputting 2P on hit only (KD Adv. +10); can juggle into MP/OD Shoryuken or SA3 in the corner

frames.name: Shoulder Throw
frames.notes: Has access to corner throw loops without walking or dashing (whiff 5LP or manually time the next throw); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

frames.name: Somersault Throw
frames.notes: Side switches; no throw/strike oki after back throwing opponent into corner; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

frames.name: Shingeki
frames.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Impact|Drive Impact]]. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

frames.name: Chest Strike (Block)
frames.notes: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

frames.name: Chest Strike (Recovery)
frames.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

frames.name: Drive Parry
frames.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Parry|Drive Parry]]. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

frames.name: Parry Drive Rush
frames.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.525 (min/throw), 1.878 (min/block), 3.628 (max/final DR frame)

frames.name: Drive Rush Cancel
frames.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

frames.name: Hadoken
frames.notes: 1-hit projectile; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

frames.name: Hadoken
frames.notes: 1-hit projectile; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

frames.name: Hadoken
frames.notes: 1-hit projectile; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

frames.name: Hadoken
frames.notes: 2-hit OD projectile; puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit

frames.name: Denjin Charge Hadoken
frames.notes: 2-hit projectile; puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit; only comes out when the HP button is pressed

frames.name: Denjin Charge Hadoken
frames.notes: 3-hit OD projectile; puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit

frames.name: Shoryuken
frames.notes: Has juggle potential; puts opponent into limited juggle state (can juggle after a high corner anti-air); cannot hit cross-up; does reduced damage and higher juggle limit on active frames 4-10 (weaker as a high-connect anti-air)

frames.name: Shoryuken
frames.notes: Has juggle potential; cannot hit cross-up; does reduced damage and higher juggle limit on active frames 4-10 (weaker as a high-connect anti-air)

frames.name: Shoryuken
frames.notes: Has juggle potential; cannot hit cross-up; does reduced damage and higher juggle limit on active frames 3-10 (weaker as a high-connect anti-air); higher knockdown advantage on the first 2 active frames (+37 refers to 1st active frame, +29 refers to anti-air with 3rd active frame; actual advantage varies based on height/distance)

frames.name: Shoryuken
frames.notes: Has juggle potential; cannot hit cross-up; only 2nd hit will connect if used too early as an anti-air; extends a combo-only hitbox to make the 1st active frame more consistent

frames.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
frames.notes: Can hit crouching opponents

frames.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
frames.notes: Only 1st hit can connect on crouching (stand blocking 1st hit forces 2nd hit to be blocked, even if crouching); later hits have better KD Advantage

frames.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
frames.notes: Only 1st hit can connect on crouching (stand blocking any hit forces next hit to be blocked, even if crouching); later hits have better KD Advantage; puts opponent into limited juggle state (can juggle afterward if 3rd hit connects)

frames.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
frames.notes: Can hit crouching opponents; puts opponent into limited juggle state; on hit, 1st hit has vacuum effect; on max range block, some hits can drop (-32 at worse)

frames.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
frames.notes: Can be input during forward jump; not an overhead; can cross-up (4f less blockstun when landing as a cross-up); at least +3 on block vs. crouching opponents; puts opponent into limited juggle state (can juggle 2HK or SA1); the last 13f of recovery puts Ryu in a crouching state

frames.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
frames.notes: Version canceled from 6HK; no follow-up juggle like after jumping Air Tatsu; the last frame of recovery puts Ryu in a crouching state; useful juggle ender after midscreen OD High Blade Kick for damage, corner carry, and oki

frames.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
frames.notes: Can be input during forward jump; cannot hit cross-up; cannot hit crouching opponents (unless they extend a vertical hurtbox), and 1st hit can only hit standing opponents on the final 3 airborne frames of jump; puts opponent into limited juggle state; the last 13f of recovery puts Ryu in a crouching state

frames.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
frames.notes: Version canceled from 6HK; can hit crouching opponents unlike the jumping version; puts opponent into limited juggle state (follow-up only possible near the corner); the last frame of recovery puts Ryu in a crouching state

frames.name: High Blade Kick
frames.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit; sets up auto-timed safe jump against cornered opponents; can combo when canceled from any normal except 5LP; the last 2 active frames have identical range (the listed best-case frame advantage is when spaced at max range, active frame 5)

frames.name: High Blade Kick
frames.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit; can be spaced safely due to its range and high active frame count; 3 extra recovery frames on whiff; the last 3 active frames have identical range (the listed best-case frame advantage is when spaced at max range, active frame 7)

frames.name: High Blade Kick
frames.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit (can follow up with 623MP, 623PP, or SA3 in corner); Punish Counter tumble state can be hit OTG; has great range and forward movement, making it a useful poke in neutral; the last 3 active frames have identical range (the listed best-case frame advantage is when spaced at max range, active frame 7)

frames.name: High Blade Kick
frames.notes: Wall Bounce into limited juggle state on hit; if close to corner, opponent flies over Ryu's head

frames.name: Hashogeki
frames.notes: Strike hitbox but can nullify 1-hit meterless projectiles; puts opponent into spinning juggle state on counter-hit/Punish Counter; excellent Burnout pressure tool (+1 on block, but increased pushback against Burnout)

frames.name: Hashogeki
frames.notes: Strike hitbox but can nullify 1-hit meterless projectiles; puts opponent into spinning juggle state on counter-hit/Punish Counter

frames.name: Hashogeki
frames.notes: 2 hits, even if it connects on final active frame; strike hitbox but can nullify 1-hit meterless projectiles; puts opponent into spinning juggle state on hit; leaves Ryu inside throw range for a strike/throw mixup on block

frames.name: Hashogeki
frames.notes: Strike hitbox but can nullify 1-hit OD projectiles or any meterless projectiles; great pressure tool

frames.name: Denjin Charge Hashogeki
frames.notes: () refers to recovery on block; strike hitbox but can nullify 2-hit meterless projectile; puts opponent into spinning juggle state on hit; only comes out when the Heavy Punch button is pressed

frames.name: Denjin Charge Hashogeki
frames.notes: 1-hit; strike hitbox but can nullify 1-hit OD projectile or any meterless projectile; crumple state is +14 before opponent becomes airborne

frames.name: Denjin Charge
frames.notes: Charges up next fireball-based move (Hadoken, Hashogeki, SA1, SA2) into a more powerful Denjin version; Ryu is in a counter-hit state for the entire animation; gains the Denjin charge on frame 50; can only store 1 stock at a time (does not persist between rounds); can also be performed after 5HP~HK or 5MP~LK~HK (on hit) by inputting 2P

frames.name: Shinku Hadoken
frames.notes: 5-hit Super projectile; button strength does not affect projectile speed; opponent cannot jump over projectile after Super freeze if within ~1/2 screen; can be preformed even when an OD Hadoken is on screen

frames.name: Denjin Hadoken
frames.notes: 8-hit Super projectile; button strength does not affect projectile speed; opponent cannot jump over projectile after Super freeze if within ~1/2 screen; consumes Denjin stock for slightly more damage and KD Advantage; can be performed even when an OD Hadoken is on screen

frames.name: Shin Hashogeki Lv.1
frames.notes: Can hold the button to charge to a higher level for more damage (in exchange for less invincibility); if button is held up to 5f after Super freeze, this invuln is extended up to the 2nd active frame (up to 18f); strike hitbox; Burnout chip damage does not changed based on charge level

frames.name: Denjin Hashogeki Lv.1
frames.notes: Can hold the button to charge to a higher level for more damage (in exchange for less invincibility); if button is held up to 5f after Super freeze, this invuln is extended up to the 2nd active frame (up to 18f); strike hitbox; Burnout chip damage does not changed based on charge level; consumes Denjin stock for slightly more damage

frames.name: Shin Hashogeki Lv.2
frames.notes: Loses 4 invincibility frames if held; invincibility can be reduced to 1-7f if the button is held the minimum length of time for Lv.2 charge (6f after Super freeze); strike hitbox; Burnout chip damage does not changed based on charge level

frames.name: Denjin Hashogeki Lv.2
frames.notes: Loses 4 invincibility frames if held; invincibility can be reduced to 1-7f if the button is held the minimum length of time for Lv.2 charge (6f after Super freeze); strike hitbox; Burnout chip damage does not changed based on charge level; consumes Denjin stock for slightly more damage; Denjin version gives the tumbling OTG state that can lead to follow-up juggles (this is the only version that incurs damage scaling in combos)

frames.name: Shin Hashogeki Lv.3
frames.notes: Loses 4 invincibility frames if held; full charge causes tumble OTG state; strike hitbox; Burnout chip damage does not changed based on charge level; extremely high pushback on block (can be difficult to punish without holding Parry into immediate Drive Rush)

frames.name: Denjin Hashogeki Lv.3
frames.notes: Loses 4 invincibility frames if held; full charge causes tumble OTG state; strike hitbox; Burnout chip damage does not changed based on charge level; extremely high pushback on block (can be difficult to punish without holding Parry into immediate Drive Rush); consumes Denjin stock for slightly more damage

frames.name: Shin Shoryuken
frames.notes: More hits connect on block vs. tall standing characters (these extra hits do not deal any additional Drive Damage on block); active frames 3-12 cause a non-cinematic hit that does reduced damage (400x4,2000); cannot anti-air cross-ups; starts up faster only when canceled from HP Shoryuken; cinematic time regenerates ~2.2 Drive bars for Ryu

frames.name: Shin Shoryuken (CA)
frames.notes: More hits connect on block vs. tall standing characters (these extra hits do not deal any additional Drive Damage on block); active frames 3-12 cause a non-cinematic hit that does slightly reduced damage (400x2,1200x3); cannot anti-air cross-ups; starts up faster only when canceled from HP Shoryuken; available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.3 Drive bars for Ryu

frames.name: Neutral Taunt
frames.notes: "Come on!"

frames.name: Forward Taunt
frames.notes: *Slaps face*

frames.name: Back Taunt
frames.notes: "Your footwork's sloppy."

frames.name: Down Taunt
frames.notes: "Come!"
